title: abronia turbinata var. carletonii
intro: Abronia turbinata var. carletonii, also known as Carleton's buckwheat senna, is a plant species that belongs to the cistanthe family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a laxative due to its high senna content. The plant's seeds, leaves, and roots are used to make herbal preparations, which can help to relieve constipation, reduce inflammation, and even lower blood sugar levels. In herbal medicine, Carleton's buckwheat senna is often used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and as a natural diuretic, making it a valuable addition to traditional remedies.
